testcase.name: test_discover_get
test_discover_head
test_notification_jsonld

failure.message: TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notification_jsonld> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4392037456'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notification_jsonld(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/notification1.json", "r") as f: attrs = {'json.return_value': json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")} mock_res.configure_mock(**attrs)
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") > self.assertTrue(1, len(notification)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()
tests/test_consumer.py:112: TypeError

testcase.name: test_notification_turtle

failure.message: TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notification_turtle> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4392221456'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notification_turtle(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "text/turtle; charset=utf-8"} with open("tests/notification.ttl", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") > self.assertTrue(1, len(notification)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()
tests/test_consumer.py:92: TypeError

testcase.name: test_notifications_jsonld_compacted

failure.message: TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notifications_jsonld_compacted> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4392160976'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notifications_jsonld_compacted(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/inbox.jsonld", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
notifications = Consumer().notifications("http://example.org/inbox")
> self.assertEqual(5, len(notifications)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()
tests/test_consumer.py:39: TypeError

failure.message: TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notifications_ntriples> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4392117072'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notifications_ntriples(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/n-triples"} with open("tests/inbox.nt", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
notifications = Consumer().notifications("http://example.org/inbox")
> self.assertEqual(5, len(notifications)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notifications_turtle> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4392180944'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notifications_turtle(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "text/turtle; charset=utf-8"} with open("tests/inbox.ttl", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
notifications = Consumer().notifications("http://example.org/inbox")
> self.assertEqual(5, len(notifications)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: object of type 'coroutine' has no len()

testcase.name: test_send_dict

failure.message: AssertionError: Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times.

testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_dict> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4392032208'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_dict(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads(f.read())
Sender().send(self.INBOX, data) self.assertEqual(dict, type(data)) > mock_post.assert_called_once_with(self.INBOX, data=json.dumps(data), headers=self.HEADERS)
tests/test_sender.py:33: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='post' id='4392032208'> args = ('http://example.org/inbox',) kwargs = {'data': '{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}', 'headers': {'content-type': 'application/ld+json'}} msg = "Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times."
def assert_called_once_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the mock was called exactly once and that that call was with the specified arguments.""" if not self.call_count == 1: msg = ("Expected '%s' to be called once. Called %s times.%s" % (self._mock_name or 'mock', self.call_count, self._calls_repr())) > raise AssertionError(msg) E AssertionError: Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times.
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:950: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_send_list

failure.message: AssertionError: Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times.

testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_list> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4391940496'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_list(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")
Sender().send(self.INBOX, data) self.assertEqual(list, type(data)) > mock_post.assert_called_once_with(self.INBOX, data=json.dumps(data), headers=self.HEADERS)
tests/test_sender.py:44: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='post' id='4391940496'> args = ('http://example.org/inbox',) kwargs = {'data': '[{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}]', 'headers': {'content-type': 'application/ld+json'}} msg = "Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times."
def assert_called_once_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the mock was called exactly once and that that call was with the specified arguments.""" if not self.call_count == 1: msg = ("Expected '%s' to be called once. Called %s times.%s" % (self._mock_name or 'mock', self.call_count, self._calls_repr())) > raise AssertionError(msg) E AssertionError: Expected 'post' to be called once. Called 0 times.
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:950: AssertionError
